zh.lifeSummary: 當Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant於大約1231年出生在法國洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯時，他的父親Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant44歲，母親Maria von Staufen31歲。他於1251年7月21日與Adélaïde de Bourgogne在比利時布拉班特洛文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1261年2月28日在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文享年31歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特洛文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant于大约1231年出生在法国洛萊姆斯布拉班吉素-斯时，他的父亲Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant44岁，母亲Maria von Staufen31岁。他于1251年7月21日与Adélaïde de Bourgogne在比利时布拉班特洛文结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1261年2月28日在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文享年31岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特洛文。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Hendrik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Henrik .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: 'de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Bright’.

en.lifeSummary: When Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant was born about 1231, in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, his father, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, was 44 and his mother, Maria von Staufen, was 31. He married Adélaïde de Bourgogne on 21 July 1251, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 28 February 1261, in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 31, and was buried in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant est né environ 1231, à Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, France, son père, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Maria von Staufen, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Adélaïde de Bourgogne le 21 juillet 1251, à Louvain, Brabant, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 28 février 1261, à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterré à Louvain, Brabant, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant um 1231 in Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant 44 und seine Mutter Maria von Staufen 31. Er hat am 21. Juli 1251 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien Adélaïde de Bourgogne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 28. Februar 1261 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien und wurde in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant è nato il circa 1231 nel Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, il suo padre, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Maria von Staufen, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Adélaïde de Bourgogne il 21 luglio 1251, nel Leuven, Brabante, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 28 febbraio 1261, nel Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolto a Leuven, Brabante, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabantが約1231年にフランスロレィヌメウゼブラバント-スル-メウゼで生まれた時，父Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabantは44歳，母Maria von Staufenは31歳であった。1251年7月21日にベルギーブラバントレウベンでAdélaïde de Bourgogneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1261年2月28日にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンで31歳で死去し，ベルギーブラバントレウベンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant(이)가 대략 1231년 프랑스 로레이인 우스 브라반트-수-우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria von Staufen(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1251년 7월 21일 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 Adélaïde de Bourgogne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1261년 2월 28일 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant nasceu aproximadamente 1231, em Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, França, seu pai, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Maria von Staufen, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Adélaïde de Bourgogne em 21 de julho de 1251, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1261, em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, com 31 anos, e foi sepultado em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant родился около 1231 г. в местности брабант-сур-меус, меус, лорреаин, Франция, его отцу по имени Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Maria von Staufen, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Adélaïde de Bourgogne 21 июля 1251 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 28 февраля 1261 г. в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 31 год и был похоронен в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hendrik III 'de Zachtmoedige' van Brabant nació aproximadamente 1231, en Brabant-sur-Meuse, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, Hendrik II 'de Edelmoedige' van Brabant, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Maria von Staufen, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Adélaïde de Bourgogne el 21 de julio de 1251, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1261, en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultado en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica.
